[
  {
    "name": "Definition of Limit",
    "formula": "\\lim_{x\\to a} f(x) = L",
    "variables": "f(x) = the function to be evaluated, a = the point to which x is approaching, L = the value to which the function approaches",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This is the definition of a limit, which is a fundamental concept in calculus."
  },
  {
    "name": "Limit Laws",
    "formula": "\\lim_{x\\to a} [f(x) \\pm g(x)] = \\lim_{x\\to a} f(x) \\pm \\lim_{x\\to a} g(x)",
    "variables": "f(x) and g(x) are functions, a is the point to which x is approaching",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law allows us to evaluate the limit of a sum or difference of functions."
  },
  {
    "name": "Product Law",
    "formula": "\\lim_{x\\to a} [f(x) \\cdot g(x)] = \\lim_{x\\to a} f(x) \\cdot \\lim_{x\\to a} g(x)",
    "variables": "f(x) and g(x) are functions, a is the point to which x is approaching",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law allows us to evaluate the limit of a product of functions."
  },
  {
    "name": "Quotient Law",
    "formula": "\\lim_{x\\to a} \\frac{f(x)}{g(x)} = \\frac{\\lim_{x\\to a} f(x)}{\\lim_{x\\to a} g(x)}",
    "variables": "f(x) and g(x) are functions, a is the point to which x is approaching",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law allows us to evaluate the limit of a quotient of functions."
  },
  {
    "name": "Chain Rule",
    "formula": "\\lim_{x\\to a} f(g(x)) = f(\\lim_{x\\to a} g(x))",

    "units": "",
    "notes": "This law allows us to evaluate the limit of a composite function."
  },

    "variables": "f(x), g(x), and h(x) are functions, a is the point to which x is approaching, L is the value to which the functions approach",
